news.t: Energy profits lift Europe earnings outlook
Eurozone Unemployment Falls Back to Record Low
Eurozone Inflation Declines as Energy Shock Fades in Relief for ECB
European stocks are poised to outdo their American peers. These catalysts explain why.
Eurozone Economic Sentiment Inches Higher After Middle East Tensions Ease

holdings.infoBox: DBEF is an equity ETF with a total of 721 individual holdings. The top holdings are Currency Contract - Usd stock at 92.40%, ASML Holding at 3.08%, Dws Govt Money Market Series Institutional Shares at 1.86%, HSBC Holdings at 1.40%, and Roche Holding AG at 1.27%.

overview.description: The Xtrackers MSCI EAFE Hedged Equity ETF (DBEF) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in total market equity. The fund tracks an index of developed-market equities excluding North America. It is hedged for currency exposure from a USD point of view. DBEF was launched on Jun 9, 2011 and is issued by Xtrackers.
infoTable: MSCI EAFE 100% Hedged to USD Net Variant

data.title: Energy profits lift Europe earnings outlook
data.text: A jump in energy company profits is set to drive European blue-chip earnings growth in the second quarter, ​overshadowing far weaker gains across the rest of the ‌market, forecasts showed on Thursday.

data.title: Eurozone Unemployment Falls Back to Record Low
data.text: The unemployment rate matched a record low in May, reflecting continued resilience despite elevated economic disruptions caused by conflict in the Middle East.

data.title: Eurozone Inflation Declines as Energy Shock Fades in Relief for ECB
data.text: Cooling energy prices helped push eurozone inflation lower in June, increasing the likelihood the European Central Bank will refrain from raising its key interest rate later this month.

data.title: European and U.S. equities post best quarter since 2020
data.text: Europe and Wall Street notch their best quarter since 2020 with tech and A.I. stocks leading the gains.

data.title: European stocks are poised to outdo their American peers. These catalysts explain why.
data.text: JPMorgan raises its year-end target for European stocks again. Panmure warns that high valuations imply negative returns for the U.S. in the next decade.

data.title: Eurozone Economic Sentiment Inches Higher After Middle East Tensions Ease
data.text: The European Commission said its economic sentiment gauge rose, driven by improving confidence in almost all sectors surveyed.

data.title: Eurozone Household Inflation Expectations Cooled in May
data.text: Inflation expectations for the year ahead among households in the eurozone fell, as consumers reacted to easing tensions in the Middle East, likely reassuring ECB policymakers.

data.title: Eurozone Consumer Confidence Picks Up Slightly
data.text: The European Commission's flash consumer-confidence indicator rose to minus 17.7 in June, continuing its tentative recovery from a three-year low in April.

data.title: EU set to remove barriers to banks' cross-border capital flows, FT reports
data.text: The European Union is set to remove barriers preventing banks from moving funds across ​the bloc, the Financial Times reported on Thursday, ‌citing a draft European Commission report.

data.text: EU-based companies will be prohibited from selling Russian liquefied natural gas next year, even ‌if buyers are located outside the EU, according to a letter seen by Reuters on Thursday.

data.text: Industrial output rose again as factories rushed to meet orders placed by customers anxious to avoid price hikes and shortages stemming from the Middle East conflict.
